channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Peru’s ex-president Toledo gets more than 20 years in prison in case linked to corruption scandal
item.description: LIMA, Peru (AP) — Peru’s former President Alejandro Toledo on Monday was sentenced to 20 years and six months in prison in a case involving Brazilian construction giant Odebrecht, which became synonymous with corruption across Latin America, where it paid millions of dollars in bribes to government
item.dc:creator: FRANKLIN BRICEÑO and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Biden is 'deeply concerned' about the release of secret documents on Israel's possible attack plans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden is “deeply concerned” about the unauthorized release of classified documents on Israel's preparation for a potential retaliatory attack on Iran, a White House spokesman said Monday.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Starbucks releasing 'Wicked' drinks inspired by the upcoming film
item.description: Starbucks is releasing a pair of limited-edition drinks inspired by two main characters of the upcoming film, "Wicked.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pinellas County man recovering after near-death experience with flesh-eating bacteria
item.description: A Pinellas County man is sharing his near death experience after contracting Vibrio Vulnificus, a flesh-eating bacteria. This comes as the Florida Department of Health is warning people to avoid floodwaters, as cases of the infection continue to rise after back-to-back hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Marino, WFLA, NBC

item.title: McDonald's says it doesn't endorse political candidates after Trump visit
item.description: McDonald's is emphasizing its political neutrality after former President Donald Trump spent some time behind the counter at one of its Pennsylvania locations.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, KRON4

item.title: Suspect accused of killing newlywed nurse had been 'deliberately cruel': report
item.description: New details are revealed in court documents surrounding an Oregon man accused of killing a nurse reported missing days after returning home from her wedding.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Footage appears to show moment helicopter crash topples radio tower in Houston
item.description: Footage from the surveillance camera shows an aircraft making contact with something in the distance just after 7:51 p.m., according to the timestamp.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Zenni Optical offering free glasses for those affected by Hurricane Helene, Milton
item.description: Zenni Optical, a Nothern California company that sells prescription glasses online, is offering free glasses for those impacted by Hurricanes Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs faces 7 new lawsuits, including alleged rape of 13-year-old
item.description: Seven new lawsuits have been filed against Sean ‘Diddy’ Combs, including one alleging the rape of a 13-year-old girl. They come as his lawyers tried again Monday to get him freed on bail, and complained that a “fresh wave of publicity” is endangering his right to a fair criminal trial.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Navy identifies 2 killed in fighter jet crash as aviators from California
item.description: The Navy identified on Monday the two crew members who died last week in a jet fighter crash near Mount Rainier as two 31-year-old aviators from California. Lt. Cmdr. Lyndsay P. Evans, a Naval Flight Officer, and Lt. Serena N.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: PHOTOS: White House public tour gets makeover with 'reimagined' visitor experience
item.description: The White House public tour is getting an extreme makeover, with new digital exhibits, an expanded route and more access to rooms poised to be part of the "reimagined" experience for visitors.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Autopsy finds 'pink cocaine' in Liam Payne's system
item.description: A partial autopsy conducted on former One Direction singer Liam Payne found multiple substances in his system when he fell to his death from the third-floor balcony of a hotel room in Argentina last week, according to a new report from ABC News.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, KTLA 5

item.title: Feds charge ex-Indian government employee in New York murder plot
item.description: “The FBI will not tolerate acts of violence or other efforts to retaliate against those residing in the U.S. for exercising their constitutionally protected rights [to free speech,]" said FBI Director Christopher Wray.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation

item.title: Biden to honor Steven Spielberg, Spike Lee, Ken Burns with National Medals of Arts
item.description: The presidential works with the National Endowment for the Humanities for the humanities medals, which were first awarded in 1997.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, NewsNation

item.title: Top Republican super PAC jumps into Nebraska Senate race
item.description: A top Republican super PAC is jumping into the Nebraska Senate race as incumbent Sen. Deb Fischer (R) struggles to put away her race against Independent Dan Osborn. The Senate Leadership Fund (SLF), a group run by allies of Senate Minority Leader Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Still using 'LOL'? That could mean you are getting old
item.description: As time has seen several follow-up iterations, including ROFL and LMAO, the original "laugh out loud" acronym is a telltale sign that who you are talking to may be a millennial.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, NewsNation

item.title: Murder suspect 'deliberately cruel' in Melissa Jubane's final moments, prosecutors say
item.description: Bryce Schubert, 27, has been charged with first-degree murder, kidnapping, and abuse of a corpse in the case of Melissa Jubane, a nurse who was found dead two days after she went missing in early September.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Plante, NewsNation

item.title: Who is Lucifer 'Justin Case' Everylove? Meet the presidential candidate who is only on Utah's ballots
item.description: Who is Lucifer "Justin Case" Everylove? Here's how this unaffiliated presidential candidate made it onto the ballots in Utah.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, Fox 5

item.title: Congress’s to-do list grows while on the campaign trail
item.description: As Congress enters a critical stretch to the November elections, a lengthy to-do list of critical funding items is piling in its absence.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Central Park Five sue Trump for defamation over debate comments
item.description: The exonerated Central Park Five sued former President Donald Trump for defamation on Monday over his comments at the recent presidential debate about their wrongful convictions for rape and assault.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KRON4

item.title: Still using 'LOL'? That could mean you are getting old
item.description: As time has seen several follow-up iterations, including ROFL and LMAO, the original "laugh out loud" acronym is a telltale sign that who you are talking to may be a millennial.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Harris raises more than $220 million in September, outraising Trump 3-to-1
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris’s campaign raised nearly $222 million in September, raising more than three times as much as former President Donald Trump’s campaign.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Protesters accuse Marine vet of White supremacy as jury selection begins in subway vigilante case
item.description: Jury selection begins Monday in the manslaughter trial of Marine veteran Daniel Penny, who was charged in chokehold death of menacing straphanger Jordan Neely.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Mexico authorities rescue hundreds after flooding strands many in high water and leaves 2 dead
item.description: Authorities in New Mexico have rescued hundreds of people in the Roswell area after record rainfall produced severe flooding over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Over 1 million voters cast early ballots in North Carolina
item.description: Nearly 1.01 million North Carolinians have already voted in the November general election, state officials reported on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, KRON4

item.title: Fethullah Gülen, US-based Islamic cleric accused of Turkish coup plot, dies in Pennsylvania
item.description: Fethullah Gülen, a U.S.-based Islamic cleric who inspired a global movement but was accused of orchestrating a failed 2016 coup in Turkey, has died in his eighties.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How does abortion translate? Ballot measures challenge interpreters
item.description: Vague or technical terms can be challenging, even more so when it comes to Indigenous languages that have only limited written dictionaries.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Blue state, ICE battle over releasing illegal immigrants as expert warns politicians put ideology over safety
item.description: An illegal immigrant accused of forcibly raping a minor in Massachusetts was released back into the community by court officials, despite an active immigration detainer against him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dodgers finish off Mets with 10-5 win in NLCS and advance to face Yankees in World Series
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani carried the injury-riddled Los Angeles Dodgers to baseball's best record during the regular season. Tommy Edman joined the team in late July and helped them reach the World Series. It's been that kind of year for the big-spending Dodgers.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Calmer weather helps contain Oakland, California, fire that forced evacuations
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Firefighting crews took advantage of calmer weather Sunday to increase containment of a small wildfire in Oakland, California, that burned two homes and forced more than 500 people to evacuate.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Republicans head to court in expanding battle about overseas ballots
item.description: Overseas voting has become the latest battlefront in Republicans’ legal challenges leading up to the election.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: One of the last Navajo Code Talkers from World War II dies at 107
item.description: John Kinsel Sr., one of the last remaining Navajo Code Talkers who transmitted messages during World War II based on the tribe's native language, has died. He was 107.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Rock & Roll Hall of Fame turns up starpower to induct new members: Who made it in
item.description: It was a five-hour-plus show on Saturday that helped induct the newest members of the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Alec Baldwin returns to 'Saturday Night Live' to spoof Bret Baier interview with Kamala Harris
item.description: Alec Baldwin has returned to “Saturday Night Live.” But the actor who won an Emmy for playing former President Donald Trump didn't reprise that role — he returned Saturday night to play Fox News' Bret Baier in an opening skit that spoofed this week's interview with Kamala Harris.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: At least 7 dead after ferry dock gangway collapses on Georgia's Sapelo Island
item.description: SAVANNAH, Ga. (AP) — Authorities said at least seven people were killed Saturday when part of a ferry dock collapsed on Georgia's Sapelo Island, where crowds had gathered for a fall celebration by the island's tiny Gullah-Geechee community of Black slave descendants.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Haddonfield, Illinois: How the iconic small town of horror in the 'Halloween' franchise came to be
item.description: Haddonfield, Illinois, a fictional small town, is the setting for John Carpenter's iconic horror film "Halloween," which has become a cultural phenomenon and has inspired numerous sequels and remakes.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, ABC 4

item.title: Where is the 'best restroom' in the U.S.? In a Utah gas station
item.description: The runners-up of this year's contest included a disco party bathroom, a bathroom full of mirrors and neon lights, and a very reflective 80s-inspired restroom in a brunch restaurant, among others.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, Fox 5
